queries: HTTP client fetch remote JSON registry

semantic_search.result_summary: Semantic search confirmed the existing files_to_modify/existing_patterns are the right targets. No additional source files were strongly relevant; the few new hits were planning artifacts (19.6 sprint docs, TD-011 records) and unrelated drift-detection code (technical-debt tooling). Two registration/persistence seams were identified from direct code inspection and added: cmd/atcr/main.go (new command registration) and internal/personas/unit.go (lock persistence during install/upgrade).

build_from.reason: client.go already owns the community-registry HTTPClient interface, the retrying fetch() helper (context timeout + exponential backoff on transport errors/429/5xx + body-size cap), FetchPersonaYAML, and FetchIndex. A new OpenRouter catalog client (models.go or similar) is the natural sibling in the same package, reusing the same HTTPClient injection seam so it is testable against httptest.NewServer with zero live network in CI, matching 19.6's discipline exactly.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add a new file (e.g. internal/personas/catalog.go) with a CatalogClient wrapping the OpenRouter /api/v1/models endpoint, reusing fetch()'s retry/backoff/timeout pattern (extract a shared helper if the URL-building differs enough to warrant one, but do not fork the retry logic). Persona binding gains a lock (resolved slug + resolved-at metadata) alongside the existing model field; Upgrade (upgrade.go:27) is extended to call the resolver before re-fetching, and the lock is what personas actually consume at review time — never the live channel token.

semantic_matches.query: HTTP client fetch remote JSON registry
semantic_matches.relevance: The existing GET-with-retry helper (context timeout, exponential backoff on transport/429/5xx, body-size cap, 404 mapping) is the direct template for a new OpenRouter catalog client — same shape, same discipline, same zero-live-network-in-CI pattern via HTTPClient injection.

This is the exact seam AC4 extends: Upgrade already re-fetches, validates, and version-compares before writing. The resolver call (family/channel -> concrete slug) belongs here, before the existing isNewer/write logic, so resolution only ever happens at upgrade time, never on the review hot path (19.6 C3 / this epic's reproducibility posture).

Existing semver-aware comparison the major-bump re-validation gate (AC6) can reuse structurally: a major-version jump (semver.Major(remote) != semver.Major(local)) is the trigger condition for the fixture-repass + re-tune-flag gate, distinct from a minor advance which already auto-writes via the current isNewer path.

The struct AC2/AC3's family/channel binding schema extends. Provider/Model are already additive (omitempty) fields per 19.6's back-compat discipline — the new logical-binding field(s) (e.g. Channel, or a structured Model that supports an alias/@stable suffix) must follow the same omitempty, permissive-decode convention so old-shape index.json payloads keep decoding.

The 10 current entries all carry a fully-qualified, non-floating model slug (e.g. anthony -> anthropic/claude-opus-4.8, glenna -> z-ai/glm-5.2 — note the z-ai/ vendor prefix, not glm/, per the epic's own advisory observation). These become the seed locks with zero migration (Proposed Solution #2).

The shared installer both init.go:47 and quickstart.go:102 call, looping over builtins.Names() (9 model-agnostic built-ins) against the fetched index (10 model-indexed library personas) — the disjoint-roster HIGH (TD-011) this epic's AC7 must close. The skip-warning fires at init.go:129.

Exact site of the 'persona %q not found in community index — skipping' warning (line 129) that fires 9 times against the real index today. AC7's fix (publish built-ins into the community channel behind a model-agnostic gate, or realign the roster with what the index actually publishes) changes this loop's roster source or the index's published set.

quickstart.go:102 makes the identical installCommunityPersonas(..., builtins.Names(), ...) call as init.go:47 — both call sites must be fixed together (or fixed once if the roster/index reconciliation moves into a shared helper) to close AC7.

The single resolution chain (project .md > registry/pinned-community .md > _base > embedded) this epic's lock must NOT touch — resolution of the PROMPT is unchanged; only the MODEL binding gains a resolver layer, entirely separate from and upstream of this chain (the lock is read by whatever assigns a model to the resolved persona, not by ResolvePersona itself).

Existing project>community>built-in tier-walking/dedup logic. `atcr models check` (AC5) needs an analogous enumeration of installed personas (not files) to report per-persona drift/deprecation, so ListTiers's dedup-by-name pattern is the closest reusable precedent even though it walks files, not model bindings.

The existing `atcr personas upgrade` Cobra command (RunE at :335, per-name call to Upgrade at :374) is what AC4 extends to report the before->after resolved slug per persona alongside the existing version-bump report — no new command, an enhanced one.

Reference for how a new `atcr models` command family should register flags/RunE/output-formatting (including a --json mode, which AC5 requires for `atcr models check`) consistently with the existing personas subcommand conventions.

semantic_matches.query: HTTP client fetch remote JSON registry
semantic_matches.relevance: A second, independent retry/backoff implementation (Retry-After-aware) for the GitHub REST API. Confirms the project's existing convention of a per-package retry helper rather than a shared one — the new OpenRouter catalog client should mirror internal/personas/client.go's fetch() (same package, same HTTPClient interface) rather than importing ghaction's variant.

The 9 built-in names (bruce, greta, kai, mira, dax, sasha, penny, ingrid, otto) returned by builtins.Names() — the disjoint side of the TD-011 roster/index mismatch AC7 must reconcile against the 10 personas/community/index.json entries.

19.6's AC7 gate enforcing YAML<->index.json field agreement is exactly what a floating channel/alias binding (AC3) must NOT break: a resolved lock's slug must still agree with whatever the gate checks, or the gate needs an additive exemption for the lock field distinct from the authored model field.

existing_patterns.name: Injectable HTTPClient + httptest zero-live-network testing
existing_patterns.description: internal/personas.HTTPClient is a minimal `Do(req) (*http.Response, error)` interface; production code passes *http.Client, tests pass an httptest.NewServer-backed client. Every fetch path (FetchPersonaYAML, FetchIndex, Upgrade) is exercised in CI with zero real network calls.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The new OpenRouter catalog client (AC1/AC8) — reuse the same HTTPClient interface and injection convention so the resolver's tests run against a checked-in snapshot fixture (AC8), never live network.

existing_patterns.name: Additive, omitempty schema extension for index.json
existing_patterns.description: 19.6 added Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags to PersonaIndexEntry with `omitempty` tags and kept the index-entry JSON decode permissive (no KnownFields/DisallowUnknownFields), so old-shape payloads decode with zero-value new fields — verified by a dedicated back-compat test (TestPersonaIndexEntry_UnknownKeysIgnored, TestFetchIndex_UnknownKeysTolerated).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Any new binding/lock/channel field on PersonaIndexEntry or a sibling lock struct (AC2/AC3) — same omitempty + permissive-decode discipline, plus an equivalent back-compat decode test.

existing_patterns.name: Fixture-gated persona content changes
existing_patterns.description: Every persona ships a committed .patch fixture (personas/community/testdata/<slug>_fixture.patch) exercised by TemplateFixtureRunner; a persona's prompt/template cannot ship or resolve without passing its fixture (C3 hard gate).
existing_patterns.follow_for: AC6's major-bump gate: 'requires the persona's fixture to still pass' reuses this exact fixture-runner mechanism rather than inventing a new validation path.

existing_patterns.name: Strict vs permissive YAML/JSON validation split by trust boundary
existing_patterns.description: Fetched/untrusted community YAML uses strict ValidateCommunityPersonaYAML (KnownFields(true)); the index-entry JSON decode path stays permissive. The boundary between 'trusted schema, strict decode' and 'evolving schema, permissive decode' is deliberate and load-bearing (see TD from 19.6 review re: upgrade.go's non-strict validation gap).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Deciding whether a resolved lock (fetched from OpenRouter, semi-trusted third-party data) needs its own validation tier distinct from both the persona-YAML strict path and the index-entry permissive path.

related_files.purpose: Owns RegistryBaseURL, HTTPClient interface, retrying fetch(), FetchPersonaYAML, FetchIndex.
Upgrade() re-fetches + version-compares + writes; isNewer() does semver comparison.
The 10 authored persona entries; current model field becomes the seed lock.
installCommunityPersonas + fetch-and-pin roster call (line 47); the TD-011 disjoint-roster site.
Identical fetch-and-pin call (line 102) to init.go; must be reconciled in lockstep.
newPersonasUpgradeCmd (line 330) and newPersonasSearchCmd (line 228) Cobra wiring; a new `atcr models` command family is registered alongside these.
ResolvePersona's prompt-resolution chain (line 47) — must remain untouched; only referenced to confirm the lock layer sits upstream of it.
ListTiers (line 53) tier-walking/dedup precedent for `atcr models check`'s installed-persona enumeration.
builtins.Names() — the 9-item roster that's disjoint from the 10-item community index (TD-011 / AC7).
A second, independent retry/backoff HTTP client for a different third-party API (GitHub) — confirms per-package retry helpers are this codebase's convention, not a shared one.
19.6's AC7 verifyCommunityIndex gate (YAML<->index.json field agreement) that a new lock field must not silently break.
Documents the index.json entry schema and contribution checklist; needs a new section for the family/channel/lock model (AC8).
Documents `atcr personas upgrade` user-facing behavior; needs the before->after lock-report update (AC4) and reproducible-vs-upgrade behavior note (AC8).
newRootCmd's AddCommand list at :202 is where the net-new `atcr models` command family must be registered.
InstallUnit and writePersonaUnit are the shared write paths for fetched persona units; any resolved lock persisted in the on-disk YAML flows through here.
Defines AgentConfig.Model — the downstream registry field that ultimately consumes the resolved lock at review time (the lock is upstream of this value).
renderAgent builds the llmclient.Invocation from registry.AgentConfig; the locked model slug reaches the wire here.
19.6's AC7 authoring-contract gate (index provider/model must equal YAML) — a new lock field must not silently break this invariant.

integration_gaps.description: No OpenRouter /api/v1/models catalog client exists in the repo; a new internal/personas/catalog.go must be created and mirror client.go's HTTPClient injection + retry/backoff discipline.
No family/channel binding or resolved-lock schema exists on PersonaIndexEntry or the persona YAML; both need additive omitempty fields with permissive decode.
init.go and quickstart.go both call installCommunityPersonas directly with builtins.Names(); there is no shared roster/index reconciliation helper, creating two-call-site drift risk for AC7.
No `atcr models` command family exists; cmd/atcr/models.go must be created and registered in cmd/atcr/main.go.
No major-bump re-validation gate exists; the semver major jump check must be wired to TemplateFixtureRunner and surface a re-tune flag before the lock advances.
No on-demand catalog snapshot refresh command exists; AC8 requires a checked-in fixture plus a way to regenerate it when OpenRouter's schema drifts.

integration_points.description: The resolver call (family/channel -> concrete slug) is inserted here, before the existing fetch/validate/version-compare/write sequence, so resolution happens exactly once per explicit `atcr personas upgrade` invocation and never on the review hot path.

integration_points.location: cmd/atcr/personas.go (new `atcr models check` command, sibling to newPersonasUpgradeCmd at :330)
integration_points.description: A net-new Cobra command reusing the personas.go registration conventions (flag parsing, cmd.OutOrStdout()/ErrOrStderr(), --json output mode matching search's pattern) for the deterministic drift report (AC5).

AC7's fix changes either the roster argument (builtins.Names()) or what the community index publishes so the two sets are no longer disjoint; both call sites must change together or route through one shared reconciliation point.
A new logical-binding field (channel/alias token) is additive to the existing struct, decoded permissively exactly like Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags were in 19.6.
The net-new `atcr models` top-level command family is registered here alongside personas, doctor, debt, etc.
Any resolved lock persisted inside the on-disk persona unit (YAML) is written through this shared paired-write tail used by both InstallUnit and Upgrade.

reusable_components.name: fetch() retry/backoff helper
reusable_components.usage_example: A new catalogFetch wrapping fetch(client, openRouterModelsURL, someSentinelErr) for the /api/v1/models call, inheriting timeout/backoff/body-cap behavior for free.

reusable_components.name: HTTPClient interface
reusable_components.usage_example: The new catalog client accepts the same HTTPClient interface so a single injected client (or a second one, if the catalog host differs from RegistryBaseURL) drives both community-repo and OpenRouter fetches in tests via httptest.NewServer.

reusable_components.name: isNewer() semver comparison
reusable_components.usage_example: Reuse semver.Major(v) comparison logic to classify a resolved-slug version bump as minor (auto-lock) vs major (AC6 gate) — the existing function already imports golang.org/x/mod/semver.

reusable_components.name: TemplateFixtureRunner
reusable_components.usage_example: AC6's major-bump gate calls the existing fixture runner unchanged to confirm the persona's template still renders under the new resolved model context; no new fixture mechanism is needed.

architecture_notes: The resolver/lock layer this epic adds is strictly upstream of internal/registry.ResolvePersona's prompt-resolution chain — it decides WHICH model string a persona binding resolves to, not how the prompt text resolves. Keep these concerns separate: no new logic belongs inside persona.go's ResolvePersona.

19.6 already proved the fetch-and-pin + explicit-upgrade discipline (C3); this epic's 'never touch the review hot path' requirement is a direct continuation of that same posture, not a new constraint to design from scratch.

Both init.go and quickstart.go independently call installCommunityPersonas with the identical disjoint roster (builtins.Names()) — fixing AC7 in exactly one place (a shared helper, or the roster/index source itself) avoids the classic two-call-site drift risk already visible in 19.6's TD history (TD-006/TD-007 were bundle/upgrade drifting from the single-install path).

The catalog spike (AC1, first task) is a hard dependency for the alias-bind design (7 of 10 personas) but NOT for the overall epic's off-ramp eligibility — the hybrid resolver's created-timestamp/explicit-pin fallback works regardless of the spike's outcome, so this is correctly scoped as an internal design decision, not a blocking gate on the whole epic.

The resolved lock has two consumers: (1) the on-disk persona unit (YAML), persisted through internal/personas/unit.go's writePersonaUnit during install/upgrade, and (2) the registry AgentConfig.Model field (internal/registry/config.go), which the fan-out reads at review time. The resolver must not be invoked on the review path; the lock is read as a static value.

19.6's AC7 gate in personas/community_test.go and internal/personas/search_test.go enforces that index.json provider/model equals the source YAML. A new lock field must either be exempt from that exact-match check or be kept in sync by the same generation path that writes index.json.

files_to_modify.reason: Add or extend catalog-fetch capability reusing fetch()'s retry/backoff.
Insert resolver call before version-compare/write; add major-bump gate logic.
Add the family/channel binding field to PersonaIndexEntry (additive, omitempty).
Seed the initial lock from each persona's current model field (per Proposed Solution #2, zero migration).
Reconcile the fetch-and-pin roster with what the community index actually publishes (AC7 / TD-011).
Same reconciliation as init.go, in lockstep.
Register the new `atcr models check` command; extend upgrade's output to report before->after resolved slug.
Register the new `atcr models` top-level command alongside the existing personas subcommand in newRootCmd's AddCommand list.
writePersonaUnit is the shared paired-write tail used by InstallUnit and Upgrade; the resolved lock (if persisted in the on-disk YAML) is written here.
Document reproducible-vs-upgrade behavior and the new models command (AC8).

files_to_create.purpose: OpenRouter /api/v1/models catalog client + hybrid resolver (alias-bind, created-timestamp newest-in-vendor-prefix, explicit-pin).
Resolver unit tests backed by a checked-in catalog snapshot fixture (AC8, zero live network).
Checked-in OpenRouter /api/v1/models snapshot fixture backing all resolver tests in CI.
`atcr models check [--json]` command: deterministic drift report (newer member, deprecation, missing slug) with exit codes.
Tests for the models check command's output modes and exit codes.

test_patterns.test_location: colocated *_test.go files in the same package (internal/personas/, cmd/atcr/)
